creator: radicleparticles@gmail.com
text:
Just as a frame-of-reference, "Linux" automatically searches for "vim-x11" when yum/dnf/apt is used to install "gvim". Even in Linux 9 versions.

I have been using FreeBSD much, much, longer than Linux.

I would just like to see a more "declartive/assertive" statement about having to choose one of the specific vim-gui packages rather than just blithely stating that they exist. That to me always indicated an option - one that I could follow or ignore. Now - it is no longer an option if one wants a (traditional?) vim gui from FBSD.
